codeQuality.notes: The implementation follows project conventions well. The getRawProfileData() method in database.ts (lines 107-114) is clean, well-documented, and follows the same pattern as existing database methods. The test file (encryption-verification.test.ts) mirrors the structure of database.test.ts with consistent beforeEach/afterEach setup/teardown, UUID-based test DB paths, and proper cleanup. The 17 tests are organized into 4 logical describe blocks covering all three verification steps: encrypted blob structure, plaintext absence, and decryption correctness. Edge case handling is thorough: unicode characters, null bio, empty strings, special characters (quotes, newlines, tabs, backslashes), long strings (10KB), and wrong-key rejection. All type check and lint pass cleanly after removing unused imports.

testCoverage.notes: 17 tests cover all required verification steps comprehensively. Encrypted blob structure: validates JSON parsing, EncryptedData field presence (iv, authTag, salt, ciphertext), and base64 validity. Plaintext absence: verifies name and bio are not in raw encrypted_data, that ciphertext contains no recognizable JSON structure, and that different profiles get different encrypted outputs (random IV/salt). Decryption correctness: round-trip encryption/decryption, null bio handling, post-update verification, wrong-key rejection, verifyProfileData method, and wrong-key verification rejection. Round-trip integrity: unicode, long strings (10KB), empty strings, special characters. Missing minor: no test for getRawProfileData returning null on non-existent profile ID, but this is a validation test file focused on encryption behavior rather than API edge cases.

integrationQuality.notes: Integration with existing code is clean. getRawProfileData() reuses the existing better-sqlite3 prepared statement pattern. Tests use the same DatabaseService, decryptObject, and EncryptedData types from existing modules. Test setup mirrors database.test.ts patterns exactly (test-data directory, UUID filenames, encryption password). No conflicts with existing profile operations, session ops, or migration system. The worker also modified only database.ts (added method) and created one new test file — no changes to crypto/encryption.ts, routes, or services, which is appropriate for a verification-only feature.

issues.description: getRawProfileData() (database.ts:114) uses `row?.encrypted_data || null` which would return null for both a missing row AND an empty string. While encrypted_data should never be empty in production, a more explicit check — `row ? row.encrypted_data : null` — would better distinguish these cases for clarity.

Test isolation in 'should reject verification with wrong encryption key' test: creates a second DatabaseService on the same file path as the test's primary db. This works with better-sqlite3 but deviates from the one-DB-per-test pattern used elsewhere.

No test for getRawProfileData() returning null on non-existent profile ID. The new file focuses on encryption behavior, but adding a simple null-return test would make the method more robustly verified.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker used .factory/init.sh for environment setup but did not add documentation about what this script does or when it should be used to AGENTS.md or .factory/library/.
sharedStateObservations.description: The init.sh script appears to be an environment initialization script (likely installing deps and setting up the database). This pattern may be useful to document in AGENTS.md or .factory/library/ so other workers know about it.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Backend worker skill file (backend-worker/SKILL.md) does not exist at the expected path. Worker used 'backend-worker' as skillName but the skill file was not found.
sharedStateObservations.description: The worker was assigned skillName 'backend-worker' but cat ~/.factory/skills/backend-worker/SKILL.md returned 'SKILL.md not found'. Either the skill is defined elsewhere or this is a missing shared state artifact.
